title: AMD Ryzen 9 9950X3D2 appears on Amazon with $1,000 pre-order price AMD confirms recommended pricing is still set at $899 [Updated]
summary: AMD's Ryzen 9 9950X3D2 is listed on Amazon at $1,000, $100 above the recommended price of $899. This discrepancy has sparked attention among tech enthusiasts and buyers. The CPU has started appearing at retailers with varying prices.
insight: Retailers are pricing CPUs above AMD's recommended retail price

title: AMD's market cap hits all-time high, Intel hits 25-year high on Agentic AI's insatiable demand for CPUs
summary: AMD and Intel stocks surge due to AI-driven CPU demand growth. The insatiable need for central processing units by AI systems is fueling a positive market sentiment. This trend is driving both companies' stocks to historic highs.
insight: AI's CPU demand fuels unprecedented growth for chipmakers

title: Quantum photonics roadmap how Xanadu and PsiQuantum are looking to transfer qubits through beams of light
summary: Xanadu and PsiQuantum are advancing quantum photonic communications through qubit transfer via light beams, paving the way for scalable quantum networks beyond 2029. Their approaches leverage the unique properties of photons to enable faster, more secure data transmission. This technology has the potential to revolutionize the field of quantum computing and cryptography.
insight: Scalable quantum networks enabled by photonic qubit transfer matter

title: Meta raising Quest headset prices due to AI-driven RAM shortage Quest 3 to cost $600, Quest 3S $350 from April 19
summary: Meta raises Quest 3 and 3S prices due to AI-driven RAM shortage. The Quest 3 will now cost $600 and the Quest 3S will be $350. This change is effective from April 19.
insight: Rising AI demand devalues Meta's affordable VR offerings

title: Pragmata PC performance tested: 18 GPUs take us to the Moon
summary: Capcom's Pragmata offers a unique blend of gunplay and puzzle solving on the Moon, with 3D printed environments. We tested 18 different GPUs for performance in rasterization, ray tracing, and path tracing. This helps players find the best hardware for a smooth gaming experience.
insight: Optimal GPU selection is crucial for a visually stunning gaming experience.

title: Chinese fabs import record volumes of US chipmaking equipment via Singapore and Malaysia homegrown tool makers booked record 2025 revenues as price competition squeezes margins
summary: Chinese chipmaking equipment firms Naura, AMEC, ACM Research, and Piotech posted record revenues in 2025.
insight: Price competition squeezes margins for Chinese chip equipment makers.

title: US appeals court restarts $3 billion patent infringement lawsuit against Intel VLSI case from 2017 returns after court sets aside 2024 decision
summary: A $3 billion patent infringement lawsuit against Intel is being reevaluated by a jury after a US appeals court found it unsuitable for summary judgment. The case originated in 2017. It will now proceed to a district court.
insight: Juries will now decide Intel's liability in patent infringement dispute.

title: Two US citizens get combined 16 years in prison for running North Korean laptop farms fake remote IT work scheme netted DPRK $5 million in around three years
summary: Two US nationals were sentenced to 16 years in prison for running a scheme where North Korean workers posed as Americans and worked at US tech firms, earning the DPRK $5 million in three years.
insight: North Korea's exploitation of US tech to bypass sanctions continues

title: Intel launches Wildcat Lake as Core Series 3 for value laptops and edge systems six consumer SKUs built on 18A promise 'all-day' battery life
summary: Intel launches Core Series 3 mobile processors, branding Wildcat Lake silicon officially. These processors promise 'all-day' battery life for value laptops and edge systems. Six consumer SKUs are built on the 18A architecture.
insight: Enhanced battery life for budget-friendly laptops and edge computing devices

title: Legendary Qualcomm, Apple, and Nuvia alumni form new CPU startup Nuvacore promises to 'rewrite the rules of silicon'
summary: Three high-profile ex-employees from Apple and Nuvia founded Nuvacore, a startup that aims to revolutionize the CPU industry by developing clean-sheet CPUs for AI workloads.
insight: AI-centric CPU design could boost efficiency in compute-intensive applications significantly.

title: Bambu updates its 3D printers to print unique hues or gradients using two or three filaments company acknowledges OrcaSlicer-FullSpectrum fork as the basis for the color prediction part of the new feature
summary: Bambu Studio V2.5.3 introduces Mixed Filament and Gradient features, allowing for unique color combinations and smooth transitions between hues.
insight: Enhanced color possibilities in 3D printing technology emerges.

title: Score 64GB of G.Skill DDR5 and a 1TB 990 Pro for an insane $441 in this Newegg bundle 9800X3D and X870E motherboard also comes with a free AIO, just $1,299 to start an AM5 gaming build
summary: Newegg offers a combo bundle with a Ryzen 7 9800X3D CPU, 64GB DDR5 RAM, 1TB 990 Pro SSD, and Asus X870E-E motherboard for $1,299.99, including a free AIO.
insight: A high-performance gaming build at a competitive starting price point

title: Asus TUF Gaming A14 (2026) review: Big CPU power in a mispriced gaming laptop
summary: The Asus TUF Gaming A14 boasts a powerful CPU but underperforms in gaming.
insight: Underwhelming gaming performance erodes its value as a gaming laptop.

title: Anonymous perps behind 86 million files scraped from Spotify hit with $322 million court judgement Anna's Archive case presents intriguing precedent for AI training
summary: A U.S. judge awarded $322 million to Spotify and labels in a default judgment against Anna's Archive for scraping 86 million files.
insight: Default judgment sets precedent for AI training data rights enforcement.

title: Non-functioning counterfeit Samsung 990 Pro SSDs are circulating in Europe Despite convincing packaging, blue PCB easily gives away the dupe
summary: Fake Samsung 990 Pro SSDs are circulating in Europe, despite convincing packaging. The clones were discovered after a buyer reported non-functioning devices in Austria. The counterfeit SSDs have a blue PCB that can be easily identified as fake.
insight: Counterfeit SSDs can cause financial loss and trust erosion in consumers.

title: Oklahoma farmer arrested and jailed for trespassing during AI data center town hall removed by officers after going a few seconds over allotted speaking time, trying to hand paperwork to counselors
summary: Darren Blanchard, an Oklahoma farmer, was arrested for trespassing during a town hall meeting for exceeding the allotted time limit to discuss concerns over a data center project.
insight: Free speech and public expression can be restricted in certain settings.

title: Virginia voter support for new data centers collapses from 69% in 2023 to 35% in new poll Multi-gigawatt, 37-building Digital Gateway project abandoned
summary: Virginia voters' support for new data centers drops from 69% to 35% in a year, indicating growing concerns about the impact of large-scale data center projects.
insight: Community opposition rises against multi-gigawatt data center development plans.

title: Struggling shoemaker and apparel brand Albird pivots to AI data centers, stock jumps 580% in a single day sells core business and leveraging $50 million in financing to become a GPU-as-a-Service and AI cloud solutions provider
summary: Albird pivots from apparel to AI data centers, sees stock surge 580%.
insight: Disruption of traditional industries by innovative tech pivots.

title: IPv6 usage reaches historic 50% across Google services, matching IPv4 increased usage eases pressure on the IPv4 address market as 'new' protocol designed in 1998 finally hits its stride
summary: IPv6 usage has reached 50% across Google services, a historic milestone. This milestone marks a significant shift towards utilizing the 'new' protocol designed in 1998. The increased usage is expected to ease pressure on the IPv4 address market.
insight: IPv6 adoption finally hits stride after nearly three decades.

title: Engineer open-sources DIY radar system that's 95% cheaper than $250,000 commercial offerings, has 20 kilometer range Moroccan engineer designs Aeris-10 radar, shares it on GitHub
summary: Moroccan engineer has designed an open-source radar system, the Aeris-10, which is 95% cheaper than commercial offerings.
insight: Affordable open-source technology democratizes access to advanced radar systems.

title: WiQwiic-32 A compact USB-C IoT board with eight Qwiic connectors (Crowdfunding)
summary: WiQwiic-32 is a compact IoT board with ESP32-S3 and Qwiic connectors for easy prototyping.
insight: Compact IoT development board for streamlined AI and ML workloads.

title: reBot Arm B601-DM An open-source 6+1 DoF robotic arm for embodied AI and teleoperation applications
summary: The Seeed Studio reBot Arm B601-DM is a fully open-source 6-axis robotic arm with a parallel gripper, designed for embodied AI learning and teleoperation applications, offering high precision and compatibility with major AI and robotics frameworks.
insight: Breaking barriers for AI and robotics research through open-source design.

title: Intel Core Series 3 Wildcat Lake processor family launched for entry-level laptops and Edge AI systems
summary: Intel launches the Core Series 3 Wildcat Lake processor family, aimed at entry-level laptops and Edge AI systems. This family features up to six cores, Intel Xe 3 graphics, and LPDDR5x/DDR5 memory interfaces. The Platform controller tile integrates various connectivity options, including WiFi 7 and Bluetooth 6.0.
insight: Intel brings AI-ready and efficient processing to entry-level devices and systems.

title: Conclusive Engineering KSTR-SAMA5D27 is an ultra-compact, low-power SBC based on Microchip SAMA5D57 SiP
summary: The Conclusive Engineering KSTR-SAMA5D27 is an ultra-compact single board computer with a Microchip SAMA5D27 Arm Cortex-A5 processor, suitable for IoT and edge computing applications.
insight: Enables efficient, low-power edge computing for IoT devices and smart systems.

title: New self-healing material can repair itself over 1,000 times, extend the lifespan of cars and aircraft
summary: A new self-healing material can repair itself over 1,000 times, extending the lifespan of cars and aircraft. This breakthrough addresses delamination in fiber reinforced polymer (FRP) materials. The new composite is designed to be tougher than traditional FRPs.
insight: Prevents structural failures, saving resources and environmental impact.

title: Japan's bullet train to debut high-tech private cabins, for an added fee
summary: Japan's bullet train to debut high-tech private cabins for an added fee, offering improved remote working conditions and internet performance.
insight: Enhancing business travel experience with exclusive high-tech amenities.

title: Memory card and flash drive pricing surges 120%, with some models spiking 260%
summary: Memory card and USB drive prices have surged by 123% compared to last year.
insight: Soaring prices impact consumers and industries reliant on storage technology.

title: Open-source tool decrypts all private data collected by Windows Recall on Copilot PCs
summary: A security researcher has released an updated tool that can bypass Windows Recall's protections, accessing private user data.
insight: Private user data remains vulnerable to exploitation by security researchers.

title: The 2026 PC and Console Gaming Report shows most revenue now comes from games outside the Top 20
summary: The PC gaming market has shifted, with titles outside Top 20 now generating most revenue. This is reflected in Western PC markets, where spending from games ranked 21 and below rose to 56% in 2025. Total PC playtime also increased by 14% over the same period.
insight: Diversification and discovery drive growth in gaming revenue streams.

title: PureMac is a new open-source macOS cleanup and app removal tool
summary: PureMac is a free, open-source macOS cleanup tool offering advanced uninstallation features.
insight: Offers a privacy-focused alternative to paid cleaning software solutions.

title: Your Airbnb host might actually be AI
summary: An AI system is being used to manage Airbnb properties, responding to guest messages, and potentially replacing human hosts.
insight: Rise of AI host management threatens traditional human host business models.

title: Intel brings 18A process to budget laptops with new Core Series 3 CPUs
summary: Intel introduces Core Series 3 CPUs for budget laptops with 18A process, offering performance gains over older PCs and previous-generation low-power Core processors.
insight: Upgrade path for small businesses and home users with significant performance boosts.

title: How Intel Got Into Trouble: We Test the Last Decade of Intel Flagship CPUs
summary: We examine Intel's flagship CPUs from Kaby Lake to Core Ultra, analyzing design choices that impacted performance and power consumption over the past decade, ultimately contributing to the company's loss of market leadership.
insight: Intel's prioritization of power efficiency led to reduced raw performance
